name: mTOR-MAPKAPK2 signaling controlled SASP transcript stability
summary: A second mTOR-SASP mechanism supports rapamycin-class senomorphic plausibility but remains preclinical.
statement: A SASP-regulator screen identified rapamycin as a potent suppressor and linked mTOR control of MAPKAPK2 translation to ZFP36L1-dependent stability of multiple SASP component transcripts.
population_or_model: Senescent cell models and SASP regulator assays
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract describes rapamycin as a potent SASP suppressor in the drug screen.
caveats: The finding is mechanistic and does not measure human aging outcomes.
mTOR inhibition has broad effects beyond senomorphic SASP modulation.
The same pathway can influence immune and cancer-relevant senescence contexts in both beneficial and harmful directions.
